_comment: Curated registry of plugin developers and their products. URL POLICY: only homepage URLs (companyname.com pattern) and verified companion-app paths/bundle IDs ship in this file by default. Download/support URLs and version-check URL+regex pairs are intentionally absent ã users add those for plugins they care about via the in-app Discover flow, and curated additions arrive through the community-additions feed. Never invent a URL here; if a homepage isn't certain, leave the developer's homepage out. Fields: identifierPrefix matches CFBundleIdentifier; productMatchers map plugin names to category info; aliases collapse copyright-string variants to a canonical name.

developerAliases._comment: Free-form aliases applied AFTER identifier-based lookup. Useful when copyright strings or weird identifiers produce a name that should be merged into a canonical developer. Keys are matched case-insensitively as substrings.

developerByName._comment: Force a plugin to a specific developer based on its name. Higher-priority than identifier-based attribution ã useful when a plugin is distributed under one vendor's bundle ID but actually belongs to a different vendor (e.g. Splice plugins with Brainworx bundle IDs).

Plugin Alliance._comment: Plugin Alliance distributes ~30 sub-brands. Every PA-managed plugin should resolve to this entry so it picks up the PA Installer companion. Identifier prefixes cover the most common sub-brand namespaces.

Lindell Audio._comment: Lindell Audio is a Plugin Alliance sub-brand (also marketed as 'Lindell Plugins'). Same companion-app relationship as Brainworx, SPL, Ampeg, etc.

Muramasa Audio._comment: Muramasa Audio is a UnitedPlugins sub-brand ã same companion-app relationship as JMG Sound / Firesonic / Instant Audio.

Firesonic._comment: Firesonic is a UnitedPlugins sub-brand ã same companion-app relationship as JMG Sound.

Instant Audio._comment: Instant Audio is a UnitedPlugins sub-brand ã same companion-app relationship as JMG Sound / Firesonic.

_appPublishers_comment: Companion apps for non-audio software vendors. These get matched against /Applications scans so that, e.g., a scanned Photoshop.app shows an 'Open Creative Cloud' button. Bundle IDs here are publicly documented and have been stable for many years.
